halftime_state.primary_context: MIN led by 4 at halftime after Victor Wembanyama was ejected at 8:39 in Q2 for Flagrant 2.
key_halftime_notes: Wembanyama finished first half/game with 12:29, 4 points, 4 rebounds, 3 fouls.
MIN had 60 halftime points but only a 4-point lead, not a true separation state.
Spurs retained enough guard creation through Fox, Castle, Harper, and Vassell to keep the game live despite Wembanyama ejection.

halftime_projection_snapshot.rebound_edge: Rudy Gobert 8+ rebounds only if available

game_side.projection: MIN win / MIN live spread edge

team_four_factor_notes.rebounding: MIN won total rebounds 49-41 and offensive rebounds 15-13.
team_four_factor_notes.second_chance: MIN won second-chance points 24-15, validating the Gobert/Reid interior path.
team_four_factor_notes.paint: SAS still won points in the paint 58-50 despite losing Wembanyama, exposing a Spurs guard-drive persistence miss in the model.
team_four_factor_notes.turnovers: MIN committed 17 turnovers to SAS 14; the third-quarter turnover cluster kept SAS alive and flipped the game temporarily.

what_worked: Correctly identified Minnesota as the strongest live side after Wembanyama ejection.
Correctly rejected full-game under exposure; final total reached 223.
Correctly highlighted Gobert rebound path; Gobert finished with 13 boards.
Projected final margin was very close: MIN projected mean +5.5, actual +5.
what_missed_or_was_underweighted: Spurs guard/wing scoring persistence after Wembanyama ejection was underweighted; SAS still scored 53 second-half points and 109 total.
Third-quarter Minnesota offensive stall was underweighted; MIN scored only 20 in Q3 on 34.8% FG with 6 turnovers.
Luke Kornet replacement value was underweighted; Kornet delivered 26:12, 9 rebounds, 2 blocks, and helped stabilize SAS interior minutes.
The model assumed Wembanyama removal would create immediate Minnesota offensive release, but Minnesota's payoff concentrated in Q4 rather than Q3.
model_diagnostic.root_cause: The halftime model was correct on side, total direction, and Gobert rebounds, but it over-penalized SAS for Wembanyama's ejection and did not fully price Spurs small-creation / Kornet stabilization. The correct edge was MIN spread, not an aggressive MIN team-total over unless the number was 112.5 or lower.
model_diagnostic.severity: LOW_TO_MEDIUM - projection profitable if primary edge was followed; diagnostic still needed because secondary team-total path was less clean.

patch_rules.trigger: Star big ejected before halftime but replacement big logs 8+ first-half minutes with positive defensive/rebounding impact.
patch_rules.rule: Do not automatically subtract full star impact from team side/total. Split adjustment into offense, rim deterrence, rebounding, and usage redistribution. Apply replacement value credit if backup center is producing rebounds/blocks/putbacks.
patch_rules.source: SAS@MIN Game 4 - Wembanyama ejected, Kornet stabilized with 9 REB and 2 BLK.

patch_rules.trigger: Team loses primary big but has at least three live ball handlers/creators with 10+ points by halftime or projected 30+ second-half combined usage.
patch_rules.rule: Maintain opponent scoring tail. Avoid hard fading team total unless pace collapses or shot quality declines for two consecutive segments.
patch_rules.source: Fox/Castle/Harper/Vassell combined carried SAS to 109 despite Wembanyama ejection.

patch_rules.trigger: Halftime team-total over relies on immediate 3Q offensive expansion after opponent star removal.
patch_rules.rule: Require first 3-4 minutes of Q3 confirmation before adding over exposure if the target team has turnover volatility or half-court spacing issues. Prefer live side over team total until scoring confirmation appears.
patch_rules.source: MIN scored only 20 in Q3 before exploding for 34 in Q4.

patch_rules.trigger: Wembanyama inactive/ejected and Gobert has 5+ rebounds by halftime with 24+ projected minutes.
patch_rules.rule: Gobert 8+ rebounds becomes playable; 10+ ladder requires plus money and close-game minutes confidence.

patch_rules.trigger: Minnesota has side edge but team scoring projection is sensitive to turnover/spacing volatility.
patch_rules.rule: Rank edge order as: 1) spread cushion, 2) rebound props, 3) team total only at discounted number, 4) avoid full-game under.
patch_rules.source: MIN side and Gobert boards hit cleaner than MIN team total over.

forward_inputs: Minnesota home/late-game response validated but not dominant; they required a 34-25 Q4 to escape after losing Q3 20-28.
Spurs remain dangerous without full Wembanyama minutes because Fox/Castle/Harper/Vassell can sustain creation.
Wembanyama discipline/ejection volatility must be tracked but not projected as recurring baseline.
Gobert/Naz/Randle offensive rebounding and second-chance pressure remain real Minnesota strengths.
Do not overreact to the Wembanyama ejection game when projecting Game 5 normal rotation. Create separate normal-rotation and disruption-rotation branches.

game5_branching.normal_rotation_branch: SAS regains Wembanyama rim pressure and closeout ceiling; Minnesota rebound edge narrows but remains relevant through Gobert/Reid.
game5_branching.foul_or_discipline_disruption_branch: If Wembanyama picks up early fouls or emotional/technical risk, activate MIN spread/rebound boost again.
game5_branching.liveflow_priority: Wait for 1H foul profile and Kornet/Wemby minute split before attacking second-half side or team totals.

betting_result_grading.recommended_primary_result: HIT if played at +3.5/+4.5/+5.5; MIN won by 5
betting_result_grading.secondary_team_total_result: Conditional; MIN actual 114 means Over 111.5/112.5 hit, Over 114.5+ failed/pushed depending line
betting_result_grading.rebound_edge_result: Gobert 8+ hit comfortably; 10+ also hit; 12+ hit if played at long odds
